# Henry Vu

> Computer Science graduate student at UT Dallas specializing in ML Systems (MLSys).
> Founding ML engineer at eXRealityAI. Research focus: efficient LLM serving,
> computer vision, and reinforcement learning. Based in Dallas, Texas.

## About

Henry Vu is a Computer Science graduate student at the University of Texas at Dallas,
focusing on Machine Learning Systems (MLSys) — making LLM/GenAI serving and training
more efficient. He graduated summa cum laude from the University of Alberta, where he
was awarded the Alberta Graduate Excellence Scholarship (AGES).

He is a founding ML engineer at eXRealityAI, building agentic voice systems and
multimodal XR applications. He is also a computer vision engineer at ThorMed Innovation,
working on NIH-funded medical imaging research (bladder segmentation using self-supervised
learning and model quantization for edge deployment).

Previously, he conducted research at Amii (Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute) on
online algorithms and multi-armed bandits, developing algorithms that are both robust
to worst-case inputs and consistent when given good predictions.

## Links

- [Portfolio](https://henryvu.io/)
- [Blog](https://www.henryvu.blog/)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/HenryVu27)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/henry-vu27/)
- [Twitter/X](https://x.com/HenryVu27)

## Current Roles

- **Founding ML Engineer at eXRealityAI** (Aug 2025): Voice-to-voice RAG system,
  multimodal XR workflows with Gemini 2.5 and YOLOv9, NLP pipeline with
  EmbeddingGemma and BAAI cross-encoder reranking
- **Computer Vision Engineer at ThorMed Innovation** (Feb 2025-Present): NIH-funded
  bladder segmentation, U-Net + SimSiam SSL pretraining on 9.2K ultrasound images,
  95.99% Dice score, 4-bit PTQ with 7x robustness improvement
- **Teaching Assistant at UT Dallas** (Jan 2025-Present): Algorithms & Data Structures

## Research & Writing

- [AI Engineering: What Fills the Context Window](https://www.henryvu.blog/) — Deep-dive on context engineering for LLM applications
- [Multi-Armed Bandits: Foundations](https://www.henryvu.blog/) — Technical foundations of bandit algorithms
- [Ultrasound Bladder Segmentation (ISBI Paper)](https://github.com/HenryVu27/ThorMed/blob/main/Thormed_ISBI_New.pdf) — NIH-funded research on SSL pretraining for medical image segmentation
- [Modeling Political Sarcasm](https://github.com/HenryVu27/PoliticalSarcasm/blob/main/report.pdf) — DistilRoBERTa fine-tuning for sarcasm detection
- [EEG Decoding: A Multi-Modal Approach](https://github.com/HenryVu27/EEG-Multimodal-Decoding/blob/main/DecodingEEGusingTransformer.pdf) — Convolution + self-attention fused with LLM embeddings for brain signal classification
- [A Survey of Geometric Set Cover](https://github.com/HenryVu27/Geometric-Set-Cover/blob/main/HenryVu_Project_CG%20(1).pdf) — Approximation algorithms and PTAS approaches

## Notable Projects

- **Agentic Framework for Suspect Detection**: Multi-agent pipeline using LangGraph and Gemini for automated suspect identification
- **Yahoo News Recommendation with MABs**: Multi-armed bandit algorithms for news article recommendation
- **ViT and Contrastive Representation Learning**: Vision Transformer with contrastive learning for image classification
- **VAE and Diffusion for FashionMNIST**: Generative models using variational autoencoders and diffusion
- **TFT Rolling Odds Calculator**: Markov Chain probability calculator for Teamfight Tactics

## Expertise

- **ML Systems (MLSys)**: Efficient LLM/GenAI serving and training, model quantization, edge deployment
- **Computer Vision**: Medical image segmentation, U-Net, SimSiam SSL, YOLOv9, OpenCV
- **Reinforcement Learning**: Multi-armed bandits (UCB, Exp3, Thompson Sampling, Gittins Index), online algorithms
- **AI Engineering**: RAG systems, context engineering, agentic workflows, LLM API integration
- **NLP**: Sarcasm detection, transformer fine-tuning, embedding models, cross-encoder reranking
- **Deep Learning**: PyTorch, Vision Transformers, VAEs, diffusion models, contrastive learning

## Education

- **M.S. Computer Science** — University of Texas at Dallas (2024-Present)
- **B.Sc. Computing Science** — University of Alberta (Summa Cum Laude, AGES Scholar)
